title: hieracium chloropsis subsp. scutifolium
intro: Hieracium chloropsis subsp. scutifolium, also known as hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family, Asteraceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, as well as its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve pain. The health benefits of Hieracium chloropsis subsp. scutifolium include its potential to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of this plant can be used to make infusions, tinctures, and teas, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
